changes_made: Removed k8s_pod_disaster_recovery_plan_documented as it doesn't map to a specific K8s resource or control
review_notes: The control CP-6(2) is applicable to Kubernetes as it relates to ensuring the availability and recoverability of the etcd data store, which is critical for cluster state management. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but 'k8s_pod_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' was removed as it does not align with a specific Kubernetes resource or control. The other functions are well-named and map to real K8s security practices.
technical_concerns: The function 'k8s_pod_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' does not map to a specific Kubernetes control or resource.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names map directly to a specific Kubernetes resource or control for clarity and implementability.
title: Alternate Storage Site - Alternate storage site | recovery time and recovery point objectives configure t
step1_reasoning: The control CP-6(2) focuses on ensuring alternate storage sites and recovery objectives. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that etcd, which stores cluster state, is backed up and possibly replicated across regions to meet recovery time and point objectives. Additionally, documenting a disaster recovery plan for pods and defining recovery objectives for the cluster aligns with the control's intent.
